findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The Flow Components slide contains a combined hydrograph chart and cross-section illustration showing riverine flow levels. The image is present in the HTML but should be verified for completeness.
findings.evidence: The PDF slide 6 shows a detailed combined graphic with a hydrograph (Discharge vs time showing Overbank Flow, High Flow Pulses, Base Flow, Subsistence Flow) and a cross-section illustration showing Valley or Channel Forming Flow, Riparian Maintenance, Channel Maintenance, Habitat Flow, Flushing Flow, and Water Quality levels. The HTML rendering includes this image.

findings.description: The Cheoah River base flow table in the PDF slide 8 is rendered as inline text in the slide image, while the HTML renders it as a proper HTML table. The data content matches, which is acceptable, but the slide image also shows the table as styled text rather than a formal table structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the table as formatted text within the slide. HTML converts it to a proper HTML table with borders and headers. Data values match: Tier A Jan=50, Feb=100, etc. and Tier B Jan=50, Feb=90, etc.

findings.description: The Flow Regime Tied to Ecology flowchart diagram from PDF slide 10 is missing as an image in the HTML. The content is converted to text/lists but the visual diagram itself is not present.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a structured flowchart diagram with boxes and connecting arrows. HTML replaces the entire diagram with nested bullet lists, losing the visual flowchart representation.

findings.description: The Flow Scenario Examples chart from PDF slide 11 showing Actual Flow, 80% of Previous Day, 80% of Weekly Average, and 20/30/40 MAF lines is reduced to just legend items in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a detailed time-series chart with 4 colored lines (Actual Flow in blue, 80% of Previous Day in red, 80% of Weekly Average in green/olive, 20/30/40 MAF in purple) plotted from 1/1 to 12/1 with y-axis up to 800. HTML only lists the legend items as bullet points: Actual Flow, 80% of Previous Day, 80% of Weekly Average, 20/30/40 MAF, without the chart image.

findings.description: In the Cheoah River High Flow table, the March Day 2 magnitude value '600' has a superscript '2' appended to it in the HTML as '6002' without proper separation, making it read as the number 6002 rather than '600' with footnote marker 2.
findings.evidence: PDF shows: March Day 2 = 600 with superscript 2 (footnote reference). HTML shows: '6002' which reads as the number 6002.

findings.description: In the Cheoah River High Flow table, the February Day 2 magnitude value 'Var' has a superscript '1' appended as 'Var1' without proper separation from the footnote reference.
findings.evidence: PDF shows: February Day 2 = Var with superscript 1 (footnote reference). HTML shows: 'Var1'.

findings.description: In the Cheoah River High Flow table header, the column header 'Magnitude (cfs)' has a superscript '3' that is rendered inline as 'Magnitude (cfs)3' in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows: 'Magnitude (cfs)' with superscript 3. HTML header shows: 'Magnitude (cfs)3 Day 1'.

findings.description: The last slide (page 12) showing the 'Flow Scenario Examples' chart is reduced to only a heading and a plain bullet list of legend items. The actual chart visualization and its axes (showing values 0-800 on y-axis, dates 1/1 through 12/1 on x-axis) are not represented even as an image tag or figure.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 12 contains a detailed line chart titled 'Flow Scenario Examples' with four data series plotted over a full year. HTML only has: '

Flow Scenario Examples

  • Actual Flow
  • 80% of Previous Day
  • 80% of Weekly Average
  • 20/30/40 MAF
' — no
